title: Introduction to Computer Science
description: MIT's legendary intro CS course — Python-based, zero experience needed, completely free with all lecture videos and assignments.

title: Learning How to Learn
description: The most-enrolled online course in history. Neuroscience of memory, spaced repetition, the Pomodoro technique — how to actually learn anything.

title: CS50: Introduction to Programming
description: Harvard's famous beginner programming course — the best intro to CS ever made, completely free. C, Python, SQL, web. Millions have taken it.

title: The Science of Well-Being
description: Yale's most popular course ever — what actually makes humans happy (vs. what we think will). Completely free to audit.

title: Khan Academy — Everything
description: Math, physics, chemistry, biology, economics, history, art — from grade school to university level. All free, all forever. The world's classroom.

title: Google Digital Marketing Certificate
description: Free Google-certified courses in digital marketing, data analytics, UX design, IT support, and project management — with real certificates.

title: Linear Algebra
description: Gilbert Strang's legendary MIT linear algebra course — the foundation for machine learning, data science, and most of modern computing. Free video lectures.

title: Introduction to Psychology
description: Paul Bloom's introductory psychology from Yale — covering memory, perception, development, mental illness, and social behaviour. Free to audit.

title: freeCodeCamp — Full Stack Web Dev
description: 3,000+ hours of free coding curriculum — HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Node, databases — with real certifications that employers recognise.

title: Photography for Beginners
description: Free photography courses covering composition, lighting, portrait, and street photography — learn to take photos that actually look professional.
